challenges.title: Three Overlapping Boxes
challenges.description: Create three overlapping boxes with different colors. Use z-index to control which one appears on top. The middle box (in DOM order) should appear on top.
requirements: Three overlapping boxes with absolute positioning
Each box should have a different background color
Use z-index to make the middle DOM box appear on top
Add labels showing the z-index value

solution.html:
z-index: 1
z-index: 3
z-index: 2
solution.explanation: Box 2 has the highest z-index (3) so it appears on top despite being in the middle of the DOM. Box 3 with z-index 2 appears in the middle, and Box 1 with z-index 1 appears at the bottom.

challenges.title: Simple Modal
challenges.description: Create a basic modal dialog with an overlay using proper z-index layering.
requirements: Overlay should cover full viewport with semi-transparent background
Modal content should appear on top of overlay
Use z-index for proper stacking
Include a title and close button

solution.html:
Modal Title

Modal content with proper z-index layering.
solution.explanation: The overlay uses z-index: 100 and the modal uses z-index: 101, ensuring the modal appears above the semi-transparent overlay.

challenges.title: Dropdown Menu with Proper Stacking
challenges.description: Create a navigation bar with a dropdown menu that appears above other page content using z-index.
requirements: Navigation bar with at least 3 menu items
One menu item has a dropdown submenu
Dropdown must appear above surrounding content
Use z-index to ensure proper stacking

solution.html:
• Home
  • Services
    Web Design Development SEO
  • About
  • Contact

Page Content

Hover over 'Services' to see the dropdown. The dropdown appears above this content thanks to z-index.
solution.explanation: The dropdown menu uses position: absolute with z-index: 100 to appear above the content. The nav has z-index: 10 to create a stacking context.

challenges.title: Z-index Scale with CSS Variables
challenges.description: Create a demo page that uses CSS custom properties for a z-index scale and demonstrates multiple layered components working together.
requirements: Define at least 5 z-index levels using CSS variables
Include: header, sidebar, tooltip, modal, and a notification toast
Each component should use the appropriate z-index variable
Show that each component stacks correctly

solution.html:
Fixed Header (z-index: var(--z-fixed))
Sticky Section Header (z-index: var(--z-sticky))
Hover me
Tooltip (z-index: var(--z-tooltip))
Toast Notification (z-index: var(--z-toast))
solution.explanation: Using CSS variables for z-index values ensures consistent layering across components. Each component uses its appropriate level in the scale.

challenges.title: Multi-Layer UI with Stacking Context Isolation
challenges.description: Build a complex UI with multiple nested stacking contexts. Include a sidebar, main content area with cards, a dropdown, a modal, and a notification system. Demonstrate how stacking context isolation works and how to fix z-index issues.
requirements: Fixed sidebar and header using z-index scale
Content cards with overlapping absolute badges
A dropdown menu that appears above other content
A modal that appears on top of everything
A notification toast that stays above the modal
Use isolation: isolate on card containers to prevent badge z-index issues
Demonstrate a z-index bug scenario and its fix

solution.html:
Complex UI Demo z-index System with Isolation

Sidebar

Fixed sidebar with z-index: var(--z-fixed)

New

Card 1

Card with absolute badge. isolation: isolate keeps badge contained.

Option 1
Option 2
Option 3
✅ Toast Notification

Modal

Modal appears above overlay. Toast stays above modal.
solution.explanation: This complex UI demonstrates: fixed header and sidebar (z-fixed), card badges with isolation: isolate (prevents badge z-index leaking), dropdown (z-dropdown), modal (z-overlay + z-modal), and toast (z-toast) that stays on top of everything.
